steps.title: Identify the topic
steps.content: The passage starts by noting that few animals spit, then focuses on a gray seal observed spitting water at an eagle. It stresses that seals had never been seen spitting before.

steps.title: Reject side details
steps.content: Choice A focuses on the observer's hobby and location. Choice C invents eagle defense behavior. Choice D suggests a comparison between species, but the text centers on the seal's behavior.

steps.title: Name the novel behavior
steps.content: Choice B matches the whole passage because it identifies the newly observed behavior of gray seals as the main topic. The answer is B.
